note: This is a local session summary export created from the current workspace context, not a raw platform transcript.
high_level_goals: Align converted-data evaluation with real project logic
Make assistant_eval runs more efficient and resumable
Reduce JSON/report noise while preserving important trace detail
Improve post-run analysis so retrieval failures are easier to diagnose

key_decisions.decision: Real chat flow should be: read profile/persona -> retrieve long-term memory every turn -> merge pending chats -> generate answer -> write pending chats -> flush when conditions are met
Priority order is current user message > relevant retrieved memories > stable user profile > assistant persona/style
Retrieved memories go into context, while current user input remains clean in user_query
ChatOrchestrator should return trace for tests/evals/debugging, but /v1/chat should not expose trace to normal end users
Scoring powers should be configurable via scoring_config; similarity uses power(GREATEST(similarity, 0), :similarity_power)
Evaluation reports should be deterministic and mode-aware, not rely on free-form LLM analysis
retrieval_eval and assistant_eval markdown reports should automatically include a retrieval failure breakdown table without requiring manual prompting

summary: Introduced ChatOrchestrator and wired it into real chat flow
Moved retrieval scoring defaults into scoring_config
Passed scoring powers through SQL path
Split reporting logic out of runner
Made JSON output more compact while keeping trace_detail
Added sample-level live persistence with in_progress/completed/interrupted status
Moved bad-case diagnosis to postprocess stage
Reused first retrieval in assistant_eval instead of retrieving twice
Added a one-off DB fixer for user_prompt_template and agent_persona_template
Shifted profile update paths toward English template generation
Added explicit language-follow rules in orchestrator prompts
Synced trace/scoring design docs with implementation

analysis_markdown_behavior: Reports are generated deterministically from result JSON
assistant_eval and retrieval_eval reports now auto-include retrieval failure breakdown
Representative cases include top1_context, missed_evidence_preview, and reason

latest_analysis_conclusion.main_problem: Retrieval is the biggest bottleneck, not answer generation.
evidence: Wrong answers were dominated by retrieval_gap
Some correct answers happened even with retrieval_hit=false, so they cannot be treated as retrieval success

remaining_follow_ups: Refine retrieval subtype rules with richer scoring trace if needed
Consider aggregating Top1 noise types in a cleaner human-readable summary
Continue splitting converted_data runner internals into smaller modules over time
Standardize long-run timeout practice around 6 hours for large assistant_eval runs
